chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – Wearing the silk and the brocade

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/335)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/335)
text: الثَّوْبِ يَكُونُ فِيهِ الْحَرِيرُ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ فِيهِ خِلْطٌ فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban, from Ismail Bin Al Fazl,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the clothes in which happens to be the silk. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If it was mixed in it, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 14. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah from more than one person from Aban from ’Isma‘il ibn al-Fadl who has said the following: “About the case of a cloth which has silk in it abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If it is mixed with other fibers it then it is not unlawful.’”
